factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly driven by commodity price volatility, which is causing margin pressure across segments. While the company has implemented measures like futures contracts and efficiency improvements to mitigate these costs, the overall impact remains challenging.

evidence.text: The principal raw materials used in our manufacturing processes are steel, copper and aluminum. In recent years, pricing volatility for these commodities and related components, including the impact of newly imposed tariffs on the import of certain of our raw materials and components, has impacted us and the HVACR industry in general. We seek to mitigate the impact of volatility in commodity prices through a combination of price increases, commodity contracts, improved production efficiency and cost reduction initiatives. We also partially mitigate volatility in the prices of these commodities by entering into futures contracts and fixed forward contracts.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Segment profit decreased $2 million in Commercial due to $7 million of higher other product costs, $5 million from lower factory efficiency, $5 million of higher commodity costs. Segment profit decreased $10 million in Refrigeration due to $3 million of higher commodity costs.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through September 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through September 30, 2018 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising input costs due to commodity price volatility and tariffs, despite mitigation efforts.

factors.summary: The end markets for Residential and Commercial heating and cooling are showing growth, while the Refrigeration segment is declining. The decline in Refrigeration is attributed to divestitures and market conditions, whereas the growth in Residential and Commercial segments is driven by higher volumes and improved pricing.

evidence.text: Results for the third quarter of 2019 were driven by year over year sales and profit growth in our Residential Heating & Cooling and Commercial Heating & Cooling segments. The Residential Heating & Cooling segment saw a 7% increase in net sales primarily on higher volumes and their segment profit was up $14 million, including the impact of insurance proceeds received for lost profits. The Commercial Heating & Cooling segment had a 7% increase in net sales and a $2 million increase in segment profit which were driven by higher volumes and higher price and mix. Sales in our Refrigeration segment decreased by 28% primarily due to the sale of our South America and Kysor Warren businesses in prior quarters, and segment profit declined $5 million.
evidence.subsection: Financial Overview

evidence.text: Residential Heating & Cooling: net sales increased 2%. Commercial Heating & Cooling: net sales increased 3%. Refrigeration: net sales decreased 27% due to divestitures.

factors.summary: Margins are under mixed pressure with higher costs impacting gross and operating margins, partially offset by pricing and cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: Gross profit margins for the nine months ended September 30, 2019 decreased 70 bps to 28.5% compared to the nine months ended September 30, 2018. Our profit margin decreased 50 bps from higher commodity costs, 100 bps from higher freight and distribution costs, 20 bps from tariffs on Chinese imports and 110 bps from other product costs. These were partially offset by 100 bps from favorable price and mix combined, 50 bps from sourcing and engineering-led cost reductions, 10 bps from favorable warranty costs, and 50 bps from the divested Australia, New Zealand, Asia, South America and Kysor Warren businesses which had lower margins.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through September 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through September 30, 2018 - Consolidated Results

evidence.text: Residential: profit increased 15.3% despite higher freight and distribution expenses. Commercial: profit decreased 1.4% due to higher costs. Refrigeration: profit decreased 17.2% due to various cost increases.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, tariffs, and foreign exchange rates, which are impacting margins and sales.

evidence.text: The demand for our products and services is seasonal and significantly impacted by the weather. Warmer than normal summer temperatures generate demand for replacement air conditioning and refrigeration products and services, and colder than normal winter temperatures have a similar effect on heating products and services. Conversely, cooler than normal summers and warmer than normal winters depress the demand for HVACR products and services. In addition to weather, demand for our products and services is influenced by national and regional economic and demographic factors, such as interest rates, the availability of financing, regional population and employment trends, new construction, general economic conditions, and consumer spending habits and confidence. A substantial portion of the sales in each of our business segments is attributable to replacement business, with the balance comprised of new construction business.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Higher commodity costs, higher tariffs on Chinese imports, unfavorable foreign exchange rates.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through September 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through September 30, 2018 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The factor highlights significant risks from commodity prices, tariffs, and foreign exchange rates impacting margins and sales.

factors.summary: The company faces near-term risks from several factors, including ongoing impacts from the Marshalltown tornado, higher commodity costs, tariffs, and foreign exchange impacts, particularly in the Refrigeration segment.

evidence.text: For the year ended December 31, 2019, we expect to receive insurance proceeds of $94 million related to lost profits and $154 million to fund the reconstruction of the facility and as reimbursement for other losses and expenses expected to be incurred. We expect to receive a cumulative total of $121 million in insurance proceeds related to lost profits and a cumulative total of $251 million to fund the reconstruction of the facility and as reimbursement for other losses and expenses expected to be incurred.

sentiment.rationale: The company faces multiple near-term risks including natural disasters, higher costs, and trade issues affecting the Refrigeration segment.

factors.summary: The company experienced significant insurance recoveries from the Marshalltown tornado, including $94 million for lost profits and $154 million for facility reconstruction, which served as a catalyst. Additionally, the company benefited from successful cost reduction initiatives, particularly in its Commercial segment, where higher price and mix contributed to improved performance.

evidence.text: In the third quarter of 2019, we incurred expenses of $3 million related to damages caused by the tornado, which included site clean-up and demolition, factory inefficiencies, freight to move product to other warehouses and sales and marketing promotional costs. We received insurance recoveries of $10 million in the third quarter of 2019 and allocated the first $2 million of these proceeds to cover our expenses and rebuilding costs incurred during the quarter and allocated the remaining $8 million for lost profits. These amounts are included in (Gain) loss from insurance recoveries, net of losses incurred in the Consolidated Statements of Operations. For the year ended December 31, 2019, we expect to receive insurance proceeds of $94 million related to lost profits and $154 million to fund the reconstruction of the facility and as reimbursement for other losses and expenses expected to be incurred. We expect to receive a cumulative total of $121 million in insurance proceeds related to lost profits and a cumulative total of $251 million to fund the reconstruction of the facility and as reimbursement for other losses and expenses expected to be incurred.
evidence.subsection: Business Overview

evidence.text: Residential: $74 million of insurance proceeds for lost profits. Commercial: $23 million of higher price and mix combined.
evidence.subsection: Year-to-Date through September 30, 2019 Compared to Year-to-Date through September 30, 2018 - Results by Segment

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company benefits from insurance recoveries and cost reduction initiatives, particularly in the Commercial segment, leading to improved performance.
